CRITICAL: 33 of 35 assertions (94%) pass for BOTH configurations — the assertion suite is almost entirely non-discriminating. Only Eval 2's 'miner leverage explanation' assertion differentiates the two skills.

Eval 1 (large-cap-tech): 13/13 pass for both. The with_skill version found IV Rank 38% and IV Percentile 80% while old_skill returned N/A for both — a meaningful quality gap that no assertion detects. Recommend adding: 'Options Analysis contains numeric IV Rank or IV Percentile values (not N/A)'.

Eval 2 (commodity-producer): The ONLY discriminating eval (11/11 vs 10/11). The improved skill's dedicated '## Gold Commodity Price Analysis (First)' section with $3,535/oz margin calculation is the clearest quality improvement. The old skill also returned N/A for all historical valuation averages while the improved skill provided actual numbers — another unmeasured gap.

Eval 3 (options-focused): 11/11 for both despite substantial quality differences. with_skill produced 70 lines of structured options analysis vs old_skill's 36 lines. However, old_skill added creative bonus sections (Risk Summary, Put-Selling Checklist) not in the template — arguably MORE useful for the specific user question.

The old skill was already strong: Both configurations produce professional-quality 150-300 line reports with real data, sourced references, and actionable recommendations. The improved skill's advantage is incremental, concentrated on stock-type-specific depth.

Assertion gap: No assertions check for (a) historical valuation data completeness, (b) number of sources cited, (c) options analysis depth beyond 5 lines, (d) whether the report adapts analysis to stock type classification, (e) explicit per-unit margin calculations for commodity producers.
Timing: Execution times are comparable (with_skill avg 285s vs old_skill avg 281s). The improved skill does not meaningfully increase time-to-completion despite more structured workflow.

Old skill's 'See the original command for complete details' broken reference did NOT prevent it from producing quality output — the model extrapolated from the framework section names and template to fill in gaps. This suggests the broken reference was not catastrophic, just suboptimal.
